title: How Small Teams Build a Reliable Podcast Production Pipeline
alt_titles: Why Podcast Production Breaks Even When the Recording Is Great
From Raw Recording to Publish: The Systems Behind a Great Podcast
description_short: Maya Chen joins the show to map out a five-stage podcast production pipeline and show how small teams can ship reliable episodes without burning out.
description_long: Consultant Maya Chen joins the show to unpack what a reliable podcast production pipeline actually looks like for a small team. We walk through the five core stages — capture, post-production, content extraction, review, and distribution — and dig into where pipelines tend to break in the real world. Along the way we cover why your pipeline is part of the product, how to separate raw and cleaned transcripts, what should always get a human review, and how to use automation to accelerate work without giving up editorial judgment.

guest.name: Maya Chen
guest.role: Advisor to media teams and creator businesses on production systems

sponsor.name: SoundLayer
sponsor.tagline: Collaboration tooling for small media teams

pull_quotes.speaker: Guest
pull_quotes.text: The pipeline is the product. If your process is fragile, the listener will feel it.

pull_quotes.speaker: Guest
pull_quotes.text: A transcript is a rich input, not the final source of editorial truth.

pull_quotes.speaker: Guest
pull_quotes.text: You don"t need an elaborate system to get meaningful gains. You need consistency.
